creative_brief: Clean, text-forward founder email. No hero image. Optional inline photo of a real partner farm (Bundarra or Collins) mid-scroll to ground the proof point. Single CTA button: 'Join the VIP waitlist' in FarmThru brand green.
subject: Buying organic doesn't fix the problem
preheader: The farmer still gets squeezed. Here's what actually changes it.
body:
Hi {first_name},

You switched to organic. Maybe you started reading labels.

But here's what nobody tells you: the farmer growing that certified organic beef still gets paid last, after the wholesaler, the warehouse, and the supermarket take their cut.

The label changed. The structure didn't.

At FarmThru, we cut every middleman between farm and hub. No wholesalers. No warehouses. Zero. Farmers like Bundarra Farm (pork), Collins Wild Caught (South Coast seafood), and Farmer Brown (eggs) get paid more because there's nobody else in the way.

But here's the thing: a grocery store built this way needs owners who actually care about it.

Not a venture fund. Not a supermarket chain buying us out in five years. People like you.

That's why we're opening a community equity round on Birchal in June. You'd become a part-owner of FarmThru, alongside the farmers and the families already ordering from us.

The minimum is small enough that this isn't a big financial decision. But it is a structural one. You'd own a piece of the grocery store that pays farmers first.

No commitment to invest today. The round opens 23 June 2026. Getting on the VIP waitlist means you see the offer 24 hours before the public, while allocation is still open.

If that sounds like where you want your values and your money pointing, join the VIP list below.

Rachel & the FarmThru team

Always consider the general CSF risk warning and offer document before investing.
